memory.summary: That scholarship money would really help, but finishing the essay alone feels heavy when I could just have some fun right now. I guess I'll skip it and go enjoy my afternoon instead.
Skipping the essay felt great in the moment, but now that the afternoon is gone, I'm already feeling a bit lighter on energy and wondering if that quick escape was worth it.
I traded the scholarship essay for immediate fun, and while it felt good in the moment, half my afternoon is already gone with the work still untouched.
Trading the scholarship essay for fun felt amazing in the moment, but now that the afternoon is fading, I'm already regretting how much time slipped away while the work sits untouched.
That fun moment felt amazing, but now that the afternoon is gone and the essay still waits, I'm already wondering if I just wasted another chunk of time.
I just pushed the scholarship deadline to tomorrow, trading a bit of future stress for immediate relief. The fun felt great now, but I know I'll have to scramble later.
The relief from ditching the essay feels good right now, but knowing I'll have to scramble tomorrow makes my stomach twist as the fun fades.
Reaching out to the hallmate felt good and kept me connected, but now I'm just waiting for tomorrow when that scholarship deadline hits again.
The immediate fun felt great and cleared my afternoon, but the relief is already fading as tomorrow's scholarship deadline looms closer.
That warm 'thank you' from my hallmate really lifted my mood, but the relief is already fading as tomorrow's scholarship deadline starts weighing on me again.
The immediate fun felt great and cleared my afternoon, but the relief is already fading as tomorrow's scholarship deadline looms closer.
My hallmate's message feels like a warm reprieve from the looming scholarship deadline, and I'm eager to grab food with them rather than face the stress alone.

desires: Not have to explain to my hallmate why I'm missing our planned meal or canceling again because I'm too stressed about the application.
Feel a rush of dopamine from doing something fun right now so I don't have to sit with the anxiety of that deadline looming tomorrow.
Keep the scholarship application unfinished just enough to feel safe, but not so much that I look completely unprepared if someone asks.

goals.description: Text back to the hallmate immediately about the food plan; I can't let them wait or they'll think I'm ignoring them, and I need that social connection right now.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive (0.5) makes avoiding friction with my hallmate more important than the looming deadline. If I don't respond instantly, it feels like a threat to our relationship. The scholarship application is just 'background noise' right now; dealing with potential awkwardness about canceling plans is a much bigger immediate stressor.

goals.description: Jump straight into the fun stream/game loop for at least an hour to get a dopamine hit and escape the anxiety of the unfinished essay.
goals.rationale: I have a short planning horizon and high procrastination bias. Sitting with the anxiety of the deadline is painful, so I need immediate relief. I'll treat this as a reward after eating with my friend, even though logically I know I should be working on the application.

agent.last_reasoning: I just sent a partial text; finishing it instantly relieves the social tension so I can finally relax. It satisfies my high affiliation drive without friction. Once they reply, I'll hop on that game stream to escape the essay anxiety.

goals.description: Jump straight into the 'fun_now' activity (game stream/hobby) immediately to break the isolation and heaviness of the scholarship essay, avoiding any guilt about skipping the application today.
goals.rationale: My current stress is moderate but my planning horizon is short; starting the heavy task feels isolating and too much effort right now. I value relationships and leisure more than this specific academic outcome at this moment, so engaging in a low-stakes fun activity preserves my social energy and mood without risking friction with anyone.

desires: To immediately start the fun activity before I lose my momentum, because sitting with the scholarship essay feels too heavy and isolating right now.
To avoid feeling guilty or 'bad' about skipping the application today, even though I know it will pile up later.
To make sure I don't accidentally mess up my relationship with my hallmate by seeming unapproachable while I'm procrastinating.
